entry.title: Re: Community Member Spotlight: Roberta Fernandes - February 2025 Edition
entry.content: Congratulations and thank you for the support.

entry.title: Creation of CAPM Project
entry.content:
Steps to Create and Deploy a CAPM Project in SAP BTP

Access SAP Business Application Studio (BAS)

  • Log in to your SAP BTP Cockpit.

  • Navigate to Services > SAP Business Application Studio.

  • Click Go to Application to open BAS.

Create a Dev Space for CAP Development

  • In BAS, create a new dev space:

    • Choose the SAP Cloud Business Application template.

    • Ensure the CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) extension is included.

    • Provide a name for your dev space.

  • Click Create Dev Space and wait for it to initialize.

Open the Dev Space

  • Once the dev space is ready, click on its name to open it.

  • The BAS interface will load, and you’ll see the workspace.

there are two primary approaches to create a CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model) project in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS):

  1. Using the Command Line Interface (CLI)

  2. Using the CAP Project Wizard (GUI-Based)

Below, I’ll explain both approaches in detail so you can choose the one that best suits your workflow.

1. Using the Command Line Interface (CLI)

This approach involves using the CAP CLI (Command Line Interface) to create and manage your CAP project. It’s a quick and flexible method, especially if you’re comfortable working in a terminal.

Steps to Create a CAP Project Using CLI:

Open the Terminal in BAS:

  • Open your dev space in SAP Business Application Studio.

  • Open the terminal.

  • Execute the command cds init my-cap-project (Initialize the CAPM Project).
  • cd my-cap-project (Navigate to the Project folder)

Using the CAP Project Wizard (GUI-Based)

This approach uses the CAP Project Wizard in SAP Business Application Studio. It’s a more visual and guided method, ideal for beginners or those who prefer a GUI-based workflow.

Steps to Create a CAP Project Using the Wizard:

  1. Open SAP Business Application Studio:

    • Log in to your SAP BTP account and open BAS.

    • Open your dev space.

  2. Open the CAP Project Wizard:

    • In the BAS interface, go to the Welcome Page (if it doesn’t open automatically, you can access it via the Help menu).

    • Under the Start from a template section, click Start CAP Application.

  3. Configure the Project:

    • The CAP Project Wizard will open.

    • Provide the following details:

      • Project Name: Enter a name for your project 

      • Template: Choose a template ( Basic CAP Project).

      • Add Sample Data: Optionally, include sample data to get started quickly.

    • Click Finish.

  4. Files and Folders Created in a CAP Project

    1. package.json

      • Defines the project metadata, dependencies, and scripts for building and running the application.

    2. cds Folder

      • Contains configuration files for the CAP server, such as cdsrc.json (custom configurations) and package.json (CAP-specific dependencies).

    3. db/ Folder

      • Contains data model files (e.g., data-model.cds) where you define your database entities and relationships.

    4. srv/ Folder

      • Contains service definition files (e.g., cat-service.cds) where you define the services exposing the data models.

    5. app/ Folder (Optional)

      • Contains UI components (e.g., Fiori elements or freestyle SAPUI5 apps) if you selected an Fiori template in the Wizard.

    6. mta.yaml (After Running cds add mta)

      • Defines the Multi-Target Application (MTA) configuration for deploying the project to SAP BTP.

    7. README.md

      • A markdown file providing basic instructions and information about the project.

    8. .gitignore

      • Specifies files and folders to be ignored by Git (e.g., node_modules/, mta_archives/).

    9. node_modules/ (After Running npm install)

      • Contains all the installed npm dependencies for the project.

    10. gen/ Folder (Generated Files)

      • Contains generated files during the build process, such as CSN (Core Schema Notation) files.

  • Add Data Models and Services:

    • Create a data model in the db/ folder (e.g., data-model.cds).

    • Define your entities and associations.

    • Create a service definition in the srv/ folder (e.g., cat-service.cds).

    • Link the service to the data model.

 

Once the project is created, Need to add model and services, after that we need to execute the project in Development using cds watch command. The cds watch command is a powerful feature of the SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) that allows you to run and monitor your CAP project in development mode. It automatically reloads your application whenever you make changes to your code, making it ideal for rapid development and testing.

What Does cds watch Do?

  1. Starts the CAP Server:

    • Launches the CAP server, which serves your data models and services.

    • By default, it runs on http://localhost:4004.

  2. Auto-Reload:

    • Watches for changes in your project files (e.g., .cds, .js, .json).

    • Automatically restarts the server when changes are detected.

  3. Logs and Debugging:

    • Provides real-time logs in the terminal, making it easy to debug issues.

  4. Service Endpoints:

    • Exposes your service endpoints (e.g., OData services) for testing.

How to Use cds watch:

open terminal: execute cds watch.

Once the server starts, you’ll see output like this:

[cds] - serving CatalogService at /catalog - impl: cat-service.js
[cds] - server listening on http://localhost:4004

Open your browser and navigate to:

  • Service Endpoint: http://localhost:4004/catalog/Products

  • Fiori Preview: http://localhost:4004/$fiori-preview (if you have a UI).

Stopping cds watch

  • To stop the server, press Ctrl + C in the terminal.

entry.title: Using HANA Cloud’s "VECTOR_EMBEDDING" Function in CAP
entry.content:
Introduction

In Q4 2024, SAP HANA Cloud introduced a feature that enables embedding generation. Previously, embeddings were obtained using SAP AI Core or external LLMs, but now they can be generated directly within SAP HANA Cloud. In this blog, I will demonstrate how to use this feature in CAP. 

 

Prerequisite

Enable Natural Language Processing (NLP) in the SAP HANA Cloud settings.

 

Basic Syntax

The following syntax is used to retrieve an embedding:

 

SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING('Hello world!','DOCUMENT','SAP_NEB.20240715') FROM DUMMY;

<text> : The text for which an embedding is created.

<text-type>: The type of the text. Must be either 'DOCUMENT' or 'QUERY'.

<model-and-version>: The name and the version of the model that is used to create the embedding.  

VECTOR_EMBEDDING Function (Vector) | Help Portal

 

Currently, only one model (SAP_NEB.20240715) is available, which generates an embedding with 768 dimensions.

 

CAP Application

I will introduce the sample CAP application I created. The code is available on GitHub.

DB schema

 

namespace cap.embedding.function;
using { cuid, managed } from '@sap/cds/common';

entity Dummy {
  key id : Integer;
}

entity Notes: cuid, managed {
  note: LargeString;
  embedding: Vector(768);
}

 

Key Points in Data Definitions

Use of the "Dummy" table

If you want to retrieve an embedding for a text that does not reside in a database table, you may want to use a syntax as below:

 

SELECT VECTOR_EMBEDDING('Hello world!','DOCUMENT','SAP_NEB.20240715') FROM DUMMY;

 

However, since CAP does not have a  DUMMY table, I created one to ensure it always contains exactly one record. I will show how to use this table in the next section.

Embedding Column Dimensions

The embedding column's dimension should be set based on to the model you use. Since we are using SAP_NEB.20240715, the dimension is 768. 

 

Service definition

 

using { cap.embedding.function as db } from '../db/schema';

service EmbeddingStorageService {
    entity Notes as projection on db.Notes excluding {
        embedding
    };

    action addNotes(notes: array of Notes) returns String;
    action deleteNotes() returns String;
    function similaritySearch(searchWord: String) returns array of Notes;
}

 

This service exposes the Notes entity and defines two actions for bulk inserting and deleting notes. Finally, the similaritySearch function returns the top three relevant notes based on the input searchWord.

 

Event Handlers

Storing an Embedding to the Database

 

   this.before(['CREATE', 'UPDATE'], 'Notes', async (req) => {
      const embedding = await this.getEmbedding(req.data.note)
      req.data.embedding = JSON.stringify(embedding)
    })

  async getEmbedding(text) {
    const db = await cds.connect.to('db')
    const { Dummy } = db.entities

    // get embedding
    const { embedding } = await SELECT.one.from(Dummy).where({ id: 1 })
                              .columns `vector_embedding(${text}, 'DOCUMENT', 'SAP_NEB.20240715') as embedding`
    return embedding
  }

 

The getEmbedding function retrieves an embedding using vector_embedding function. Since the result is an array, it must be converted to a string before storing it into the database.

 

Performing a Similarity Search

 

    this.on('similaritySearch', async (req) => {
      const searchWord = req.data.searchWord
      const embedding = await this.getEmbedding(searchWord)
      
      const db = await cds.connect.to('db')
      const { Notes } = db.entities

      // retrieve relevant notes
      const notes = await SELECT.from(Notes)
                                .columns('ID', 'note')
                                .limit(3)
                                // .where`cosine_similarity(embedding, to_real_vector(${JSON.stringify(embedding)})) > 0.7`
                                .orderBy`cosine_similarity(embedding, to_real_vector(${JSON.stringify(embedding)})) desc`
    
      return notes                    
    })

 

This function first retrieves an embedding for a given search word, then executes a similarity search to get the top three relevant notes.

 

Testing

The following notes are stored in the database.

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Notes",
  "value": [
    {
      "ID": "02e197c9-4f06-4abf-b314-37d89226dfb0",
      "note": "Remember to buy milk, eggs, and bread on the way home."
    },
    {
      "ID": "31ccffee-e186-4ec8-90c2-cfdee5e9a77f",
      "note": "AWS EC2 instance needs to be restarted after applying updates."
    },
    {
      "ID": "8a78b77d-7210-465d-861f-f2d4ff249371",
      "note": "Read 'The Lean Startup' by Eric Ries for innovation insights."
    },
    {
      "ID": "9841088a-d5b8-44ac-8841-e7f3909d1cc8",
      "note": "Meeting with John on Monday at 10 AM about project roadmap."
    },
    {
      "ID": "d65a1f36-ede1-4808-9bfc-a38f1a7dc888",
      "note": "Flight to San Francisco departs at 3:45 PM on April 12th."
    }
  ]
}

 

Now, let's perform a similarity search using the search word "shopping list".

 

### Similarity Search
@searchWord = shopping list
GET {{server}}/odata/v4/embedding-storage/similaritySearch(searchWord='{{searchWord}}')

 

The following three records are returned, with the most relevant appearing at the top.

 

{
  "@odata.context": "$metadata#Notes",
  "value": [
    {
      "ID": "02e197c9-4f06-4abf-b314-37d89226dfb0",
      "note": "Remember to buy milk, eggs, and bread on the way home."
    },
    {
      "ID": "9841088a-d5b8-44ac-8841-e7f3909d1cc8",
      "note": "Meeting with John on Monday at 10 AM about project roadmap."
    },
    {
      "ID": "d65a1f36-ede1-4808-9bfc-a38f1a7dc888",
      "note": "Flight to San Francisco departs at 3:45 PM on April 12th."
    }
  ]
}

 

 

Conclusion

With the VECTOR_EMBEDDING function, you can retrieve embeddings without needing SAP AI Core or external LLM services. However, in real-life RAG scenarios, LLMs are still required. Consider this a starting point and extend it to fit your specific use case.

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA 2022 System Conversion Steps & Details – How to be prepared?

entry.title: Re: Configuring the SuccessFactors Employee Central > Advance Types and Deductions

Neatly presented, I have one question. How the system will behave if an employee goes on unpaid leave?

 

Regards

Siddharth

entry.title: Cash Mgm: how exclude FOC Sales Order flow
entry.content:
Hi Gurus, 

I'd like to know how to avoid free of charge sales orders in FQM_FLOW table.

I mean,  some SD scenarios generate sales order that will not be posted in Accounts Receivable, only taxes in GL Account, like: sample, bonus, plant transference  etc. 

These scenarios should not be displayed in Cashflow because it's not a liquidity forecast. 

I've  already reviewed all FSCM customizing and search a sap notes.

Could you please help me.

Thanks in advance. 

BR

Att

RL

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA: Inventory Management Tables New Simplified Data Model (NSDM)
entry.content: Very useful and a great post!

entry.title: Re: Issue with Admin Role Permissions in Enable Now
entry.content:
Hi Jaja,

When you create a User Role in Manager, the Administrator that creates the Role is automatically added as the only person who can then assign the Role to other users.  To fix the issue:

1. Open Manager and log in as an Administrator
2. Select Administration > User Roles
3. Click on the User Role to be modified
4. Click the Manage Access button
5. Either remove all Roles from the Selection field or add a Role that will allow the Administrators to assign the Role
6. Click OK

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: 10 Steps to Clean Core for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition for Customers
entry.content:
Hello Thomas,

Is that 10 members cap per company is flexible? or is it okay if we can get the combination of resources up to 10 at any point in time.

Cheers

Sagar Pilli

entry.title: WFA on HANA dimension labels
entry.content:
Hi @YSE-1 

I have an issue with a few custom pretty basic dimensions - 1 level flat org related like legal entity, dep etc.

I have 4 of those, all with quite a few results that come as "??" which would mean they do not have labels.

But strangely enough they do, I have checked them in manage data as well as canvas reporting and I can find both en_GB and en_US descriptions.

Here is the SQL:

select DISTINCT IFNULL(CAST(JOB_INFO."DEPARTMENT" AS nvarchar(50)), '??') AS Level1Id, 
IFNULL(CAST(DEPARTMENT_LABEL."LABEL" AS nvarchar(50)), '??') AS Level1Name
 
from ( SELECT DISTINCT EMP_JOB_INFO_T."DEPARTMENT" DEPARTMENT FROM "[%ODS_DATABASE%]"."EMP_JOB_INFO_T" ) JOB_INFO
 
LEFT OUTER JOIN "[%ODS_DATABASE%]"."MDF_SPLIT_GENERIC_OBJECT_T_Department" DEPARTMENT_CODE ON JOB_INFO."DEPARTMENT" = DEPARTMENT_CODE."INTERNAL_ID" AND CAST(DEPARTMENT_CODE.EFFECTIVE_END_DATE AS date) = '9999-12-31' 
LEFT OUTER JOIN "[%ODS_DATABASE%]"."CONFIG_GENERIC_OBJECT_T_GOLocalizedData" DEPARTMENT_LABEL ON DEPARTMENT_CODE."EXTERNAL_NAME" = DEPARTMENT_LABEL."INTERNAL_ID" AND DEPARTMENT_CODE.ROW_ID = DEPARTMENT_LABEL.PARENT_ROW_ID AND DEPARTMENT_LABEL."LOCALE" = 'en_GB' 
 
 
 
ORDER BY Level1Name

Here is a screenshot of my results for one of the dimensions:

Kind regards,

Alex

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Balance Validation - errors in new fu...
entry.content: Thanks for the reply. I have setup as per the instructions and can run the new validation tasks ok, however I still receive the error message in the consolidation monitor and can not see the consunits in the assignment drop down as per my original issue.

entry.title: Mass output management - FI accounting
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We have a requirement for our current client, who is using an S/4HANA Public Cloud package, to automatically generate forms to be sent via email to customers, based on FI invoices.

Is it possible to achieve this automation and mass management through the Fiori app "Output Parameter Determination" (please note that we are not using FI-CA)?

The requirements are as follows:

  • Form generation must be automatic upon FI invoice creation.
  • Mass form generation and sending are mandatory.

Best regards,

Constant

entry.title: Re: LMS Search for Library-No libraries were found with t...
entry.content: @Tam check on what Domains are the libraries sitting as there might be Domain restrictions

entry.title: Re: SuccessFactors Learning best practices for file namin...
entry.content:
There is no best practices for naming conventions unless you have multiple Domains and restrictions So to keep the check ex A Company- AITEM,Aclass

Hope this helps

entry.title: Re: Group Reporting Balance Validation - errors in new fu...
entry.content:
Here are the steps to Configure Balance Validation

1.Activate the Feature Toggle Enable the feature toggle for the Balance Validation frontend (FINCS_BV_ADAPTATION) in the configuration activity Activate Features, based on the Balance Validation help documentation.

2.Manage Validation Rules In the Manage Balance Validation Rules and Rule Groups app, you can display, modify, and create validation rules and rule groups. Ensure that they are activated for validation.

3.Assign Rule Groups Use the Assign Rule Groups for Balance Validation app to assign rule groups to consolidation units and groups. This depends on the execution category setting defined in the Define Balance Validation Tasks configuration activity. The assignment is version-dependent and applies to a specific special version and period.

4.Execute Validation Once configured, you can use the Data/Consolidation Monitor to execute and monitor balance validation.

entry.title: Re: S4HANA Cloud Create Outgoing Invoices (FB70) - how to...
entry.content:
Hi AndreasMuno,

I've checked the SAP note and it does not meet my requirements since I can't see the item long texts of previous journal entries.

I'll close this question because no existing function is available for that requirement.

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: Re: HTML Application is not appearing in the HTML Applica...
entry.content: That's usually an indication that your managed app router is not configured correctly. I would check the xs-app.json file, the destination definition in the mta.yaml.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for SAP on SAP MaxDB
entry.content:
I would to like to know, if the numbers of volumes in MaxDB has an impact on performance for a SAP BW instance.  Which case would give a better performance using the latest MaxDB engine 7.9.11 

Case 1)   Number of Volumes = 70;  size of each volume = 4 GB

Case 2) Number of Volumes = 30 ; size of each volume = 8 GB

I assigned a good size of memory to the engine and no issues with it; I just would like to know how could I put performance further  

In advance, thank you for your commens/suggestions

For the DS repo migration, please refer below
https://me.sap.com/notes/2768127

Best Regards,
Joy

entry.title: Work Zone Two-Multifactor Authentication - Activate TOTP Two-Factor Authentication
entry.content:
We are currently testing the various options available for Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) in IAS, and we would like to enable the One-Time Password (OTP) via SMS.

Could you please provide us with the best practices and steps to activate SMS-based TOTP Two-Factor Authentication?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.content:

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-identity-services/cloud-identity-services/apps-settings-configure-sinch-service-in-administration-console

entry.title: Re: Test Plan Execution Stuck at "Enter Application&...
entry.content: @praveenjha The issue has occurred again, and the executing test plans are blocked once more. I’d appreciate it if you could take another look at this.

entry.title: Re: Access Completed I9 Form
entry.content:
Hello @Dheeraj05 ,

1. I am guessing this is for ONB2.0. After completion of section 1 and section 2, you should be able to see the submitted documents through the Onboarding documents in the actions of the ONB dashboard.

OR 2.  you can also find these documents through manage documents as this is the document repository and you can find every document that you have uploaded in the SFSF instance. You might have to play around with the document name field in this screen with a few letters of the candidate first name, last name. But this is a 100% sure shot to get the documents from SFSF instance.

If you are using a document repository like Open text then you can find the documents there as well.

Hope this is helpful. Good luck!

Regards,

Surya

entry.title: Re: How do i calculate the data between the manage systems and Solution Manager
entry.content: We helped you to accept the answer, since there were no updates after 14 days. You can unaccept it anytime if the answer provided was not helpful enough and you have further questions.

entry.title: How To Create A Basic Custom Entity?
entry.content:
If you're working with SAP and want to fetch data from different sources without directly connecting to a database, Custom Entity is your go-to option. It helps you get data from APIs, web services, or other custom business logic without much hassle.

In this guide, we'll go through step-by-step instructions on how to set up a Custom Entity in Eclipse.

What is a Custom Entity?

A Custom Entity is like a virtual table in SAP. But instead of storing data, it pulls information from different sources whenever you need it. This makes it perfect for showing dynamic data or connecting with external systems.

Why Use Custom Entities?

  • No Database Needed: You don't need to create database tables.
  • Flexible Data Sources: Fetch data from APIs, services, or other systems.
  • Great for Reporting: Use it to display data without saving anything.
  • Works with CDS Views: You can combine it with other SAP data models.

Steps to Create a Custom Entity in Eclipse

1. Preparing Your Development Environment

First, make sure you have:

  • Eclipse IDE with SAP Development Tools (ADT) installed.

  • Access to your SAP BTP or ABAP system.

2. Building the CDS Data Model

In Eclipse:

  1. Open your ABAP Cloud Project.

  2. Right-click onCore Data Services, then choose New Data Definition.

  3. Name your entity and use this simple template.

 

@EndUserText.label: 'Custom Entity Example'
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@OData.publish: true

define custom entity ZC_CUSTOM_ENTITY {
  key ID   : UUID;
  Name     : String(50);
  Age      : Integer;
  Country  : String(50);
}

 

This code creates a custom entity with fields like Name, Age, and Country. The ID field is the unique key.

3. Defining Custom Entity Behavior

Now, let's tell SAP what the Custom Entity should do. Create a Behavior Definition file with this code:

 

managed implementation in class ZCL_CUSTOM_ENTITY_HANDLER unique;

define behavior for ZC_CUSTOM_ENTITY persistent {
  use draft;
  field ( mandatory ) Name;
  field ( readonly ) ID;
  determination getData on modify;
}

 

  • Makes the Name field mandatory.
  • Marks ID as read-only.
  • Sets up a rule to fetch data whenever something is modified.

4. Implementing Business Logic

Now let's create the handler class where we fetch the data:

 

class ZCL_CUSTOM_ENTITY_HANDLER definition
  public final create public .

public section.
  interfaces if_rap_query_provider.

endclass.

class ZCL_CUSTOM_ENTITY_HANDLER implementation.
  method if_rap_query_provider~select.
    data lt_data type table of ZC_CUSTOM_ENTITY.
    append value #( ID = '123456' Name = 'John Doe' Age = 30 Country = 'USA' ) to lt_data.
    append value #( ID = '789012' Name = 'Jane Doe' Age = 28 Country = 'Germany' ) to lt_data.
    result = lt_data.
  endmethod.

  method if_rap_query_provider~select_by_association.
    " Custom implementation for associated queries
  endmethod.

  method if_rap_query_provider~select_by_key.
    data lt_data type table of ZC_CUSTOM_ENTITY.
    if keys is not initial.
      loop at keys assigning field-symbol(<key>).
        if <key>-id = '123456'.
          append value #( ID = '123456' Name = 'John Doe' Age = 30 Country = 'USA' ) to lt_data.
        elseif <key>-id = '789012'.
          append value #( ID = '789012' Name = 'Jane Doe' Age = 28 Country = 'Germany' ) to lt_data.
        endif.
      endloop.
    endif.
    result = lt_data.
  endmethod.

  method if_rap_query_provider~select_count.
    result = 2.
  endmethod.

endclass.

 

  • select: Fetches all records.
  • select_by_key: Fetches specific records by key.
  • select_by_association: Allows fetching data through associations (relationships between entities).
  • select_count: Returns the number of available records.

5. Testing Your Custom Entity

  • Right-click your project and choose Run As > ABAP Application.
  • Use SAP Gateway Client or Fiori Elements to check if your Custom Entity is returning data.

Conclusion

This simple yet powerful feature helps you fetch dynamic data without setting up database tables. If you need to get data from APIs or other systems, Custom Entities are a great option.

Let me know if you'd like any help or have questions.

entry.title: Re: Free Form Payment Request – Release Dual Control
entry.content:
Hi,

I checked following documentation about the Setup Guide for Free Form Payment Approval using SAP Workflow Management or SAP Build Process Automation,

https://hub.sap.com/odata/1.0/catalog.svc/Files('a80efbcf-8f15-4b77-acba-eca5d37f4f49')/$value#:~:text=SAP%20users%20can%20use%20My%20Free%20Form%20Payments,variants%20can%20be%20configured%20in%20a%20no-code%20approach.

Hope it helps.

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: The Field "DONOTCHECKMATERIAL" not available in the Billing document request ODATAV4 API
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We have a requirement to create the billing document using external data using API's. So we are trying to implement the External billing document request  ODATA V4 API. while analyzing the entities and structure, we are not able to find the field  "DONOTCHECKMATERIAL". this is a critical requirement as they don't have materials for billing purpose. please check and guide us with the possible solution.

The API references are as follows :
Overview | Billing Document Request | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

The reference from the help portal :
Billing Document Request | SAP Help Portal


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The Field "DONOTCHECKMATERIAL" not available in the B...
entry.content:
Dear customer,

The field DONOTCHECKMATERIAL is only available for SOAP API.

Here below are the available APIs for you to consider which contain the field DONOTCHECKMATERIAL.

Billing Document Request - Create | SAP Help Portal

Billing Document - Create | SAP Help Portal

Best regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: SAP S 4/HANA Guidance
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!! My name is Ayanna Duncan. I am a recent SAP S/4HANA graduate from Vower, Inc. I will soon earn my certification in SAP S/4H ANA Cloud Public Edition. I am a highly motivated individual with a strong work ethic, and excited to apply my skills and knowledge. I have a background in business management, document control and healthcare informatics. I'm currently in Northern Virginia but will strongly consider North Carolina. I’m confident that my positive attitude, eagerness to learn, and strong work ethic will make me a strong asset to any team. I look forward to connecting with other SAP professionals for guidance and/or opportunities. Thank you

email: adduncan2001@yahoo.com

ご都合がつく方は積極的にオンサイトでの参加をお待ちしてます。
次は、3月14日です！
-------------------------------
We are happy to be able to start SIT Tokyo again this year.
The presentations and atmosphere on Day 1 were fantastic.
We look forward to seeing everyone who can make it on-site.
The next event is on March 14th!

entry.title: Re: subcontracting BOM components in PO do not generate r...
entry.content: Is the component set up as phantom assembly (special proc. key 50 or similar) ? And uses planning strategy 'Planning at Phantom Assembly" (strategy 59 or similar)? In this case have a look at the documentation for the planning strategy and reconsider your settings for the components and your planning process.

entry.title: Re: Personalized Recommendation - SAP BTP
entry.content:
Apologies for the late reply but better late than never!

There is a slight error in the request payload where the "boosting_attributes" values should be a dict instead of a list.

The correct request payload should be:

{
  "user": "8",
  "items_ls": [
    "49"
  ],
  "boosting_attributes": {
    "categoricalFeatures": {
      "categories": {
        "values": [
          "Drama",
          "Comedy"
        ],
        "weights": [
          0.2,
          0.4
        ]
      }
    }
  },
  "k": 3,
  "explain": true
}

entry.title: Re: Mass output management - FI accounting
entry.content:
Hi Constant,

Yes, it is possible to achieve automatic form generation and mass management for FI invoices in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud using the "Output Parameter Determination" Fiori app. Here's how you can meet your requirements:

  1. Automatic Form Generation:

    • Use the Output Parameter Determination app to configure output parameters for FI invoices [1].
    • Set up the output type for email delivery and specify the appropriate form template [1].
    • Configure the system to automatically trigger form generation upon FI invoice creation [1].

  2. Mass Form Generation and Sending:

    • Utilize the batch processing capabilities of the Output Management system [2].
    • Configure the dispatch time as "Send with periodically scheduled job" to enable mass processing [2].

  3. Email Delivery:

    • Switch the output medium from print (Medium 1) to external send (Medium 5) [2].
    • Maintain email addresses in the customer master data (ADRC table) [2].
    • Update output condition records to use external send (condition type 05) [2].

  4. Avoiding Email Spam:

    • Instead of sending individual emails for each invoice, set up batch processing to consolidate multiple invoices into a single email per customer [2].

  5. Custom Templates:

    • If needed, you can register custom form templates using the Output Management Form Template app [3].
    • These custom templates can be associated with specific output parameters in the Output Parameter Determination app [3].

  6. Segmentation:

    • You can configure output parameters to segment invoice distribution based on criteria such as plant, material, or other relevant factors [3].

By implementing these configurations, you can achieve automatic form generation upon FI invoice creation and manage mass form generation and sending through the Output Parameter Determination app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud.

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA dimension labels
entry.content: Hi @abalasescu2 - I think it is not only the label that has issue but it can also issue with the Department not listed or available in the MDF object.  Both of these will output ?? as a result.  Have you checked if the department in the Job Info is available or not in the MDF object?

entry.title: SAP BTP应用开发扩展线上课程
entry.content:
由SAP Adoption Service Center大神带领大家每天四小时的实战课程

注意要自备环境：Cloud Foundry Runtime, SAP Business Application Studio 或者 SAP Build Code, SAP HANA Cloud 或者 PostgreSQL on SAP BP - hyperscaler option, SAP Build Work Zone - standard edition)

entry.title: Re: 在执行付款策略后，形成清算的银行科目后，后续如果过过账到总账对账单中，或者说导入对账项目后，如何形成冲销清算科目
entry.content:
1. 在S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition中,付款处理和银行对账的一般流程如下:
  • 执行付款策略后,会形成借记应付账款,贷记银行清算科目(如11001020)的凭证 [1]
  • 导入银行对账单后,系统会自动处理银行对账单,将贷项过账到银行主科目(如11001000),同时借记银行清算科目(11001020),完成清算科目的冲销 [1]
  • 如果未能自动冲销清算科目,可能需要检查以下几点:
    • 确保银行对账单中有对应的行项目 [1]
    • 检查银行主科目和清算科目的主数据设置是否正确 [2]
    • 审核自动清账的配置是否正确 [2]
    • 还需要参考3的内容进行银行对账单自动清账未清项的配置
  1. 关于J78 Scope Item:

J78 是与银行账户管理相关的 Scope Item。但即使未启用 J78,上述基本的付款和对账功能仍可实现,因为这属于核心财务流程 [2][1]。

  1. 相关的 Scope Item:
  • BFA: 基础银行账户管理
  • J77: 高级银行账户管理

这些 Scope Item 涵盖了银行管理和对账单处理的相关功能 [2][1]。

总之,付款和银行对账是S/4HANA Cloud的标准功能。通过正确配置和操作,可以实现清算科目的自动冲销。如果遇到问题,建议检查具体配置和数据设置。

entry.title: How to create sales order from Excel
entry.content:
Hi,


How to create sales order from Excel ?
Please help

Thanks !


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to create sales order from Excel
entry.content:
In the SAP Business ByDesign system, in the Sales Orders view, click New, then Sales Order from Microsoft Excel.

You can decide what you want to do with the template. Choose one of the following options:

  • If you want to use the template only once, you can open the template without saving it. Click Open.

  • If you want to save the template so that you can use it again, select the required save option under Save.

(Note: If you have previously downloaded and saved this template on your computer, navigate to the location where you have saved the template, and open it.)

Log on to the solution from Microsoft Excel

  1. In the SAP Add-In ribbon in Microsoft Excel, click Logon.If the SAP Add-In ribbon is not displayed, check to make sure that the Add-In for Microsoft Excel has been installed correctly (see Prerequisites in this document). A dialog box opens where you can enter the logon details. The system URL is proposed automatically. The system URL is the URL of the system that you are working with.

  2. Enter your user ID and your password, and click Logon.

Enter Details in the Microsoft Excel Template

Note the following:

  • The Microsoft Excel template is presented with a number of rows where you can enter or copy your data. If you need more, add the number of rows you need before you start entering or copying your data.

  • Ensure that mandatory fields (those marked with an asterisk) are filled.

  • To help you fill in the details:

    • Some fields have dropdown lists.

    • In some fields you can search the system for data, for example, countries. Place the cursor on the field, and click Lookup in your solution's tool bar or ribbon to search the system. A search field is available in the Lookup dialog box that appears. When you start to type text in the search field, the relevant entries are filtered in the ID and Description columns, meaning that you do not have to scroll through the whole list. If the Lookup button is not active in the ribbon or toolbar, then it is not possible to perform a search.

Enter the sales order details in the rows, and in the appropriate columns.

Save Your Data

  1. After you have finished entering all the data, save the Microsoft Excel file.

  2. Click Save Data to.

    A dialog box opens, informing you that the data is being saved to the solution.

    After the upload, a message informs you that your data has been saved in the solution.

  3. You can then log off by clicking Logoff.

The new sales order is saved in the SAP Business ByDesign system and added to the list in the Sales Orders view.

If you need to make changes to these sales order, you must change it directly in the system.

entry.title: Re: Trigger creation of delivery documents in SAP TM
entry.content:
Hello Michael,

Here is my setup:

  1. Has to two sales orders with "Order Combination" checked.  
  2. I have a delivery profile with settings of "Per Freight Unit".
  3. When I create a Delivery from Freight Order, I was expecting to create two deliveries (based on Delivery Profile rule), but system kept creating only one delivery.

What would be reason system ignoring "Per Freight Unit" profile setting ? 

-Thanks

Ramesh Gojji

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere, SAP BW bridge: Demystifying the Remote Conversion
entry.content:
Hi Felipe

Thanks for the informative post. Trying to understand the basics right here,had 2 followup questions to further analyze.

1) Migrating from SAP BW/4 HANA (with Hybrid Modeling) to SAP Datasphere?

Does SAP BW Bridge support Calc Views Migration? Or do we need to recreate them in SAP Datasphere?

2) BW Query & BO Reports when moving to SAP Datasphere? Does BW bridge support these query elements or do we need recreate these in SAC?

Thanks

KP

entry.title: Re: "What's New in Q4 2024 - Using RFCs and BAPIs in SAP Build Process Automation"
entry.content:
Hi Community,

" To help you extend/utilise RFCs from On-Premises solution, you can now create Actions based on RFCs / BAPIs in Process Automation. Utilizing the BAPI from library, you can directly incorporate RFC actions, streamlining the integration process. "

 

Thanks for your selfless sharing, it really help us a lot.

🙂

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Credly certificate
entry.content: I have completed the BTM3 learnings but have yet to receive the credly certificate.

entry.title: Three Reasons to Read the SAP Business Data Cloud E-Book
entry.content:
Last month, SAP announced SAP Business Data Cloud, a fully managed software-as-a-service (SaaS) solution that unifies and governs all SAP data and seamlessly connects with third-party data.   

As an evolution of our industry-leading data, planning, and analytics solutions, SAP Business Data Cloud brings together SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, and SAP Business Warehouse with a unified experience that delivers transformational insights across all lines of business.   

SAP also announced a landmark partnership with Databricks that brings the power of Databricks directly into SAP Business Data Cloud. This marks a new era in enterprise data management, with two leaders in their domains coming together to redefine how applications and data platforms work together. 

Our new e-book, Unleash Transformative Insights with SAP Business Data Cloud, offers valuable insights into the product, including more information about the core features, architecture, and use cases.  

Here's why you should check out this e-book: 

1. Deep Dive into Core Features 

Explore the inner workings of SAP Business Data Cloud and discover how it can fuel every part of your organization with harmonized data. This e-book provides an overview of its core functionalities, from insight apps to data products. By mastering these features, you'll be equipped to drive your business forward with data-informed decisions. 

2. Visualize the Architecture 

The e-book includes an architecture diagram of SAP Business Data Cloud, illustrating how the various components work together to deliver a unified data foundation. This visual guide will help you comprehend the architecture's complexity and appreciate how it unifies SAP Datasphere, SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP Business Warehouse, and SAP Databricks into a cohesive ecosystem. 

3. Explore Real-World Use Cases 

One of the most valuable aspects of this e-book is the real-world use cases it presents. Find out how different lines of business can leverage SAP Business Data Cloud to unleash transformative insights, connect data, and foster reliable AI, including:  

  • Core enterprise analytics: Monitor financial health, refine cash flow, and improve payment terms for agile decision-making. 
  • People analytics: Evaluate workforce trends, track talent flows, and align HR and business strategies to boost engagement and productivity. 
  • Spend analytics: Gain visibility into spending, mitigate risks, and optimize procurement for maximum value. 

Read the E-Book 

Discover how an integrated approach to data management delivers the context and insights to help businesses make actionable decisions quickly. Download the Unleash Transformative Insights with SAP Business Data Cloud today and learn more about harnessing the power of semantically rich data.

Thomas

entry.title: FCLM_BAM_MAINT537 not available in SAP Area S4hana NWBC
entry.content:
Dear Expert,

In NWBC, Manage Bank Account, I woud like ti assign multiple house bank account to one central house bank. User need this because of in one House Bank, they have three Bank account number.

SAP suggest to change message control FCLM_BAM_MAINT537 from error message to warning message through spro-financial supply chain management -cash and liquidity management-bank account management-basic setting. But after check there was no FCLM_BAM_MAINT537 not available ion those area.

Kindly advice

Thanks

Puput

The GitHub provides an easy-to-follow central entry point for SAP BTP content. Stencils in DRAW.IO is also a great open-source tool. Appreciate the effort in making complex information more visually appealing and accessible to users. Well done! "

 

🙂

Thanks for your kindly sharing, it really help us a lot.

 

Regards,

ZFiori.

entry.title: Full Time Employee Planning - Calculation of FTE YTD
entry.content:
Hello all, 

I’m encountering an issue while calculating Full-Time Employee (FTE) Year-to-Date (YTD). My dataset includes only two years: 2018 and 2019.

Initially, I calculated FTE Actual using the following formula:

DRV0220 FTE Actual:LOOKUP([DRV0100], [d/VERSION].[p/VERSION] = "Actual" and [d/TIME] = "201906")

However, when implementing this in the SAC Account dimension, I had to make some adjustments to get the correct values, using:LOOKUP([DRV0100], [d/VERSION].[p/VERSION] = "Actual" and [d/TIME] = "2019"."20192")

Similarly, for calculating Opening Initial, I initially used:

FTE0010 Opening Initial:IF([d/TIME].[p/CALMONTH] = "201906", [DRV0220], 0)

But in SAC, I had to modify it as follows to get the expected results:IF([d/TIME].[h/YQM] = ("2019", "2019"."20192", "2019"."20192"."201906"), [DRV0220], 0) The issue arises when applying the final logic for FTE YTD:

DRV0210 FTE YTD: LOOKUP([DRV0200], [d/TIME] = Current("Year").LastPeriods("Month", 24))No values are appearing. I’m unsure what mistake I might be making. Could you help identify any necessary adjustments? I've attached a screenshot for reference.

entry.title: Material flow rules tab missing
entry.content:
How to maintain Material Flow of a Site.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Material flow rules tab missing
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Hope you are doing good.

You are unable to maintain the material flow for a Site via Edit Layout view, please kindly edit the Site first and then maintain material flow of it.

  1. Go to Supply Chain Design Master Data - Locations view.
  2. Search for site D2000.
  3. Click on Edit button.
  4. Then you can see Materiel Flow subtab.

Thanks and have a nice day!
 
Best regards,
Aurora
SAP  Support

entry.title: Re: How to prevent new pricing performed when Item catego...
entry.content: As per your previous confirmation, to avoid the re-determination of pricing in the Public Cloud, you need to go to the copying control and set the Pricing Type field to 'D' (Copy price elements unchanged). However, it seems that this step has now been denied.

entry.title: How to include Project System in Make-to-Stock process in Public cloud?
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am currently working with a client who wishes to integrate Project System into the Make-to-Stock process. As I have not yet had experience with this specific scenario, I would greatly appreciate your guidance on the process.

Your insights and support would be extremely valuable to me.

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: User Managment IAS/Azure
entry.content:
Hello @yogananda 

Thank you for your explanation, it clarifies the process well.

But there are situations where, for example, a user stays in the company and should remain in Azure AD, but their access to SAC should be revoked.

I cannot deactivate this user in Azure AD (since they would lose access to other applications in Azure). If I remove user ABX from the sac_admin group, they will also be deleted from SAC, and their content will be lost.

Do you have any tips on how to proceed when access to SAC needs to be revoked, but the user's content in SAC and the user in the IdP should remain?

Is there a way to manage this more effectively in Azure AD or SAP IAS ?

 

Many Thanks

Best Regards

entry.title: How to include Project System in Make-to-Stock process in Public cloud system?
entry.content:
Dear Team,

I hope you are doing well.

I am currently working with a client who wishes to integrate Project System into the Make-to-Stock process. As I have not yet had experience with this specific scenario, I would greatly appreciate your guidance on the process.

Your insights and support would be extremely valuable to me.

Thank you in advance for your help.

Best regards,
Souhardhya Sen

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: No item displayed in My Inbox in Workzone
entry.content:
Symptom

I have enabled My Inbox in SAP BTP Work Zone. However, when workflows are triggered, no items appear in the My Inbox. 


Environment

  • SAP BTP Work Zone

  • My Inbox Application

  • SAP Build Work Zone, Advanced edition

                                                                     Added the Recipient 

                                                                             Trigger Details


                                                             Assigned Processor

                         

                                                           No Visible Items in the Inbox



I am facing an issue in above and have attached a screenshot for reference. If anyone has encountered a similar issue and found a solution, I would glad if any assistance you do. Thank you in advance for your support!



SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry runtime and environment 
SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition , SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Credly certificate
entry.content: Is it BTP learning or BTM3 learning?

entry.title: Re: Need WBS ELEMENT in start condition of Manage workflo...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please make sure below points to let the search help work:

1. Make sure the entity exist in the service path.

2. Make sure the entity (parameter entity ) , property (parameter-property) has maintaine 'Valuelist' annotation.

Please check below example:

CONSTANTS: co_id TYPE if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>ty_condition_id VALUE 'CF_SWF_FLEX_IFS_BADI_COND_SAMP'.

data : ls_condition like line of ct_condition.
data : ls_parameter like line of ct_parameter.

* WBS Element Condition
ls_condition-id = 'id_WBS'.
ls_condition-subject = 'WBS Element'.
ls_condition-type = if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>cs_condtype-start_step.
append ls_condition to ct_condition.

* Parameter for Workflow level condition - WBS Element
ls_parameter-id = 'id_WBS'.
ls_parameter-name = 'WBS Element'.
ls_parameter-xsd_type = if_swf_flex_ifs_condition_def=>cs_xstype-string.
ls_parameter-service_path = '/sap/opu/odata/sap/FAC_GL_DOCUMENT_POST_SRV'.
ls_parameter-entity = 'FinsPostingGLItems'.
ls_parameter-property = 'WBSElement'.
ls_parameter-mandatory = abap_false.
append ls_parameter to ct_parameter.

 

Best regard, Take

entry.title: Re: Purchasing group not auto populating in Purchase requ...
entry.content: Thanks Johannes. The problem was because of the wrong entries in table MMPUR_V_MG2PGRP. Especially duplicate entries. After removing its working fine now.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud get SSL Handshake error while calling API
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I call an API by postman with get method success,

https://cpx.cbc.gov.tw/API/DataAPI/Get?FileName=BP01D01

 

but get exception while using abap code in s/4hana cloud as below

--> SSL handshake with cpx.cbc.gov.tw:443 failed after 421

 METHOD if_oo_adt_classrun~main.

    " Create HTTP client; send request
    TRY.
        DATA(lo_destination) = cl_http_destination_provider=>create_by_url(
            'https://cpx.cbc.gov.tw/API/DataAPI/Get' ).

        DATA(lo_http_client) = cl_web_http_client_manager=>create_by_http_destination( i_destination = lo_destination ).
        DATA(lo_request) = lo_http_client->get_http_request( ).

        lo_request->set_query( query = 'FileName=BP01D01' ).

        DATA(lo_response) = lo_http_client->execute( i_method = if_web_http_client=>get ).
        DATA(lv_json_results) = lo_response->get_text( ).

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_exception).
        out->write( lx_exception->get_text( ) ).
    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

How can I do to solve this issue.

entry.title: Re: How to retain the original file name in SAP PI/PO in...
entry.content: That is not enough - you also have to enable this parameter:

entry.title: Re: How to retain the original file name in SAP PI/PO in...
entry.content:
You have to set the ASMA attributes explicitly in the Channel:

If you are changing from FTP (File Adapter) and SFTP, please be careful, the parameter looks the same, but they are differing in lower/capital letters. In the monitoring you can see what has been set and then you can read them in your mapping either via XSLT-JAVA extension or if you use graphical tool, you have to code an UDF.

BR Helmut

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI - process direct adapter - Async mode - Fire...
entry.content:
I think I have found the solution for this, you can use Escalation end in exception subprocess. This will make sure you achieve below 3 things which you wanted.

- Parent/Caller iflow will be marked as 'Completed' even though there is an error occurred in child iflow.

- Child iflow will be marked as 'Escalated' in case error occurred in the child iflow and you will get to see the error in message monitoring.

- Yo can still use Process direct adapter for your both the integrations.

I have implemented this in our integrations. PFB screenshots for your reference.

 

Hopefully this helps.

Regards,

Jayesh

entry.title: Re: Requirement to Verify Group Journal Entries

Hi Vishwakarma,

Thanks for the quick response.

Since we are in S/4 Hana Cloud, we might need data sources to create report. Can you please recommend some data sources.

Regards,

Abha Hardikar

Kathiravan S

entry.title: Does shipping method rules affect promotion discount?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I have a promotion "100% Discount on the delivery mode 'ground-net'". Sometimes we noticed that delivery cost is added to the totals despite the promotions got applied to the cart. 

Does shipping method rules like 'cutOffTime' affect promotion discount?

entry.title: Re: Ensuring Data Consistency in the Extraction of SuccessFactors Data to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hi @Miki922 ,

Remote table approach is certainly a solid and effective solution for many use cases, especially when you need real-time access to data, minimize storage requirements, and capture data at specific points in time. It helps to get a Snapshot on a daily basis for the future data, but to get the Snapshot of the historical data and to bring data from multiple data sources with advanced business logic we may still need Dataflows to complete the process.

Thank you,

Divya

entry.title: Usage of Catalogs, Code-groups & Codes in EAM Notification
entry.content:
Introduction:

Catalog is the customizing master data from Qulity Management (QM) application & in this blog I shall explain how the same is used in SAP Enterprise Asset Management application (henceforth it will be called as EAM) to describe the equipment’s problems through „code“ format (key that uniquely identifies the qualitative contents of a problem) in the „notification“.

Catalogs are created in the golden/customizing client & copied to other clients. It is used in various business processes of EAM to capture information related to maintenance history in the form of codes. Each code is assigned to a code group in the catalog. You can assign codes to different code groups. Catalog code and descriptions can be stored in a catalog.

There are many catalog codes delivered by SAP & we use only the following 5 catalog codes in the EAM application:

Sr.No.

Business Description

Catalog Codes

 SAP Description

1

Problems/Failures

C

Damage Overview

2

Causes of Damage

5

Causes

3

Tasks to be done

2

Tasks

4

Activities Completed

A

Activities (PM)

5

Sub – parts

B

Object Parts

Apart from these standard code groups, users are free to create own code groups as per their requirements in customizing client.

All the codes illustrate why an asset failed during production, what is the cause for that failure, tasks to be performed & activities done to overcome that failure. The use of codes in a SAP system ensures a consistent way of documenting the key aspects of the damage, cause, events etc. according to pre-defined categories. Few examples of „codes“ for „cause“ code-group are:

  1.         Breakage
  2.         Corrosion
  3.         Imbalance
  4.         Misalignment
  5.         Overheating
  6.         Vibration       

Catalog profile will group different characteristics of the notification, which will cater the needs of management to analyse the company assets & to ascertain the decisions accordingly. Catalog profiles have sub-class of multiple codes groups which will group/merge multiple codes as per their features. This is used for the damage analysis.

Transactions:

QS41 & 43: Maintain Catalog. Create new code groups & codes.

QS42: Display Catalog.

Working example:

Catalog codes are maintained in customizing – SPRO:

TA: S_ALR_87001010_1: Create own catalog & maintain the same. Catalog names 0 to 9 & A to O are used/assigned by SAP. You are free use catalog types P to Z.

Next step is to create code groups & codes under the catalog in TA: QS41 & maintain them. Example shown here is for catalog type „object parts“  (B).

Status of the code group should be „released“ to use.  

Now, maintain the codes for the created code-groups.

In this example, we created catalog codes, code groups & codes only for the „object parts“ code-group. Similarly, create codes for all the code-groups of all the catalog-codes.

Next step is to assign the created catalog to catalog profile & in-turn assign the catalog profile will be assigned to „notification“ type so that these codes will be displayed in the notification screen (TA: IW21) only of that notification type.

Maintaining catalog profile is done in SPRO:

User can use the TA: OQN6 for the same.

Assign the catalog & its code-group to catalog profile.

Note that you can assign any code groups (which is assigned to other catalog’s also) & a catlog profile can have multiple catalog codes.  

Assign this catalog profile to notification type (M1) in the SPRO:

 

Notification type can have only one notification profile.

Let’s see how it works while creating the notification (type M1).

Use TA: IW21 to create a notification with type M1.

You can see the values of F4 help of Object Part (catalog code = B): the one which we created in earlier steps & assigned (PM1 missed out) to catalogs profile will be displayed. The additional sub-screen „item“ section in the notification home screen & also the new tabs: „item“, „task“ & „activities“ can be added as shown in below.

To accommodate these (screen) changes in the notification screen, you need to change or add the new section in the customizing of notification type:

For the tab01, add new „item“ section.  Include other new tabs for items, task & activities.

Kindly note that these catalogs & code groups can be assigned only to „notification“ in plant maintenance.

Reports

Earlier LIS reports: MCI5 & MCI7 are used to analyse the data w.r.t code-groups & codes. In SAP S/4 HANA, SAP recommends to use the SAP Fiori app „Technical Object Damages“ for the same.

Migration:

These catalogs can be migrated (migration object: S4_QM_CATALOG_CODE) to SAP S/4 HANA system using the BAPI: BAPI_QPGR_SAVEREPLICA. Required authorization object is Q_CAT_GRP.

entry.title: sampling scheme ISO sampling templates
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

Previously, my colleague tested the sampling scheme in the quality management module of the ByDesign product, and the system could directly apply the ISO sampling templates.

May I ask if S/4HANA Public Cloud also provides similar templates that can be applied?

Below is the ByDesign screen for reference.

Thank you!

Please let us know the event date. I also wanted to check if you still accept speaker nominations and how we can reserve our slot. I look forward to your response.

Thank you!

Regards,
Sravya P

entry.title: Re: How to use a single position for multiple employees?
entry.content: Thank you Manish

entry.title: Re: How to use a single position for multiple employees?
entry.content: I did the same, however, the field while creating position is not visible is what I am highlighting

entry.title: Re: Date calculation in TM Freight Order/Booking
entry.content: Hi @SFC, "Can you please clarify whether the second screenshot is a freight booking?" -> Schedule Master Data For Road. Kind Regards,

entry.title: unable to find SAP CAR table for replication in SAP datasphere
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a standalone SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT) system running on DMIS 2018. I have created a new Mass Transfer ID connected to an SAP Customer Activity Repository (CAR) system running on a HANA database. However, when creating a new replication flow in SAP Datasphere, I am unable to see any tables available for replication. I have previously created a similar Mass Transfer ID connected to an SAP R/3 system running on an ABAP stack, and I never encountered any issues with that setup.

Do you have any recommendations on how I can resolve this issue?

Thanks,

Bhat

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP SETUP for BUILD APPS
entry.content:
Easy to follow and understanding.
Thanks,
Venkata viswam Revilla.

entry.title: Restriction for changing specific Material Master data?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We would like to know if there is a way to restrict authorization to change specific Material master data items (YB-0000 and YP-0000).

We have checked the restrictions for business roles but it seems impossible to allow changes for only certain material master items.

Could you give us some advice on this?

Best Regards,

Fujita

entry.title: Re: Enable 2FA for SAP PaPM V3 On-Premise
entry.content:
Hi Leonardo,

concisely the answer is yes, but this is not a PaPM-specific topic, rather something related to the authentication to the on premise SAP system.
For instance you can achieve this with SAP Single Sign On or SAP Cloud Identity Services.
See this link as an example: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SINGLE_SIGN-ON/ec58da89706946a6ac63e348c27f00a0/0623094809b5422184394e1498dbdbb0.html?version=2.0

I suggest you to reach out to your trusted SAP security experts for more information and to discuss your situation.

Hope it helps
Emanuele

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content: Hi all, it feels great to be here. I started my journey as an SAP Technical Consultant September 2023, I am already certified in Database Administrator - SAP HANA and SAP Integration Developer. Looking forward to connecting with you all! 🙂

entry.title: Re: 在执行付款策略后，形成清算的银行科目后，后续如果过过账到总账对账单中，或者说导入对账项目后，如何形成冲销清算科目
entry.content: 导入银行对账单行项目处理： 1.形成了客户KH0001应收账款61020.00元； 2.形成可供应商GYS0001付款策略中形成的银行清算科目11002020.00金额24408.00元 3.进入管理银行对账单APP后，进行相应的对账单行项目导入， 4.导入系统过账后，未能形成冲账； 5.系统未能形成自动冲账，冲销对应的应收账款或者银行清算科目，即借：银行存款 贷：应收账款或借：银行清算科目 贷：银行存款

entry.title: Re: Restriction for changing specific Material Master data?
entry.content:
AFAIK you cannot for individual material id.

Check blogs such as How to restrict access to material types and material groups in SAP S4 HANA  using authorization objects M_MATE_MAR (Material Master: Material Types) and M_MATE_WGR (Material Master: Material Groups). Consier creation of some specific group for those items?

entry.title: Re: Seamless planning between SAP Analytics Cloud and SAP Datasphere is coming! Controlled release i
entry.content:
Hi @Max_Gander  Customers who are stuck on the old SAC infrastructure and therefore cannot use seamless planning. If we were to move to BDC would this mean that our SAC tenant would automatically run on the new infrastructure and therefore also enable Seamless Planning?

 

BR Frederik

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price changing after procurement of fr...
entry.content: I think batch management is the right tool to manage samples from different suppliers.
